note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
SHOCK measurement of the CME based on the source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery.
Measurement of the leading edge of the bulk of the CME based on potential source location. STEREO A COR2 imagery was not available for this measurement.

BULK measurement based on best fit in SOHGO LASCO C2/C3 and longitude precedent set by a series of similar CMEs triangulated between longitudes -120 and -130 degrees likely indicating a complex active region near this longitude.
SHOCK measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and approximate source location of the bulk measurement.
Measured longitude based on the source location. The measured parameters may vary slightly as this event is too faint to be observed in STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and limited STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measured longitude based on the source location. The measured parameters may vary slightly due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based loosely on the source location, taking into account the observed southern deflection of the eruption with respect to the on-disk source location. There is no stereoscopic viewpoint available for this event due to an ongoing campaign in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured parameters may vary as a result.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 imagery and one frame of STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to an ongoing STEREO A campaign limiting the amount of usable imagery, and the far-sided source, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Measurement of the CME using one spacecraft before the diffuse front disappears in LASCO C2.
Leading edge measurement using only SOHO LASCO C2 and EUV signatures to approximate the source latitude and longitude.
Parameters based on potential source location. Longitude may vary by +/- 10 degrees. There is a higher degree of uncertainty due to limited stereoscopic viewpoints of this CME.
One spacecraft leading edge measurement using the known source to approximate the latitude/longitude as STEREO A imagery is not usable due to a campaign.
Leading edge measurement of the CME out to 18 solar radii using SOHO LASCO C3 and knowledge of the source location using EUV imagery.
One spacecraft leading edge measurement which results in speeds close to the observed type II shock speed but decreased in velocity with farther frames, potentially due to deacceleration.
Leading edge associated with the fainter and faster portion directed more southeast as viewed from SOHO LASCO using triangulation with STEREO Ahead.
Measurement of the leading edge using two spacecraft.
Preliminary measurement with early frames of the CME using one spacecraft and knowledge of the source location seen in EUV imagery. Uncertainty in latitude about +15/-5 degrees.
Measurement based on approximate source location and  best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
Parameters are based on apparent source location and best fit of features using SOHO LASCO imagery. There is not STEREO A COR2 imagery available for this event due to a data gap.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 using the source for longitude. There is no STEREO A COR2 imagery available for this event due to a data gap.

Parameters based on fit with the visible front and the potential source location. The half-width may vary due to uncertain left side boundary of CME front. Uncertainty is high due to no stereoscopic viewpoints of this CME.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The CME has an irregularly-shaped front which is difficult to measure, and a wider half-width was used to accommodate the leading edge shape.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, noting the CME deflecting southward from the source based on how it travels through the coronagraphs, complicating swpc_cat's tracking of the front direction. However, this fit is close to the estimated source as observed in STEREO EUVI imagery, with visual triangulating getting worse if you go further beyond W95.
Very uncertain analysis of the faint wider front (not the brighter narrow one), based on the fit of faint fronts in two coronagraphs and on the assumption that the source is Active Region 3938.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, noting the CME is much fainter in COR2A running difference than COR2A white light. Using the white light imagery as a reference, the CME was matched to the running difference within swpc_cat, allowing a good confidence triangualtion around W65.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, finding a good far-sided high southern latitude fit despite the lack of an observed source in GOES SUVI imagery.
A very uncertain one-coronagraph (SOHO) analysis based on the assumption that the source is the eruption in Active Region 3939.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 to triangulate the source, which suggests higher confidence in the northern hemispheric eruption as opposed to AR 3939 in the southeast.
Measurement of the brighter, bulk portion of this event based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement of the faint, outermost SHOCK feature based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location.
Bulk measurement of the CME using SOHO C2; the leading edge becomes very diffuse with time.

Measurement of the faint shock front using triangulation between STEREO A and SOHO; simulation is likely to overestimate geomagnetic impacts at Earth given there is not much observed material behind the leading edge.
Analysis based on fit with two coronagraphs (since no clear source was found on disk).
Analysis is based on the fit with two coronagraphs but also incorporates the location of the likely source of this CME, the flare.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. This event is not being simulated as it fades after a few frames in SOHO LASCO C2.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A. This front appears to be slightly less than a halfwidth of 10, and also triangulated slightly further on-disk than the source observed in GOES SUVI imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. While in SOHO the fuzzy front can be traced over multiple timestamps, in STEREO A COR2 a super faint front to the SW is seen only in two timestamps.
Very uncertain one-coronagraph analysis with SOHO coronagraph only based on the assumption that minor activity in Active Region 3936 is the source of this CME.
Analysis based on a fit with two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not possible. The measured parameters may vary slightly as this measurement does not take into account possible deflection from the source.

SHOCK measurement of the faint, outermost feature based on the source location. This measurement may vary as there is no STEREO A COR2 imagery to confirm the measured parameters. This measurement does not take into account possible deflection from the source location.

Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not possible. The measured parameters may vary slightly as this measurement does not take into account possible deflection from the source.

Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not possible. The measured parameters may vary slightly as this measurement does not take into account possible deflection from the source.
Measurement based on source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2. Measurement made with only early C2 frames due to ongoing SOHO and STEREO data gaps.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on probable source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Measurements give a possible range in longitudes from ~W110 to ~W140.
Measurement based on likely source location visible in GOES SUVI.
One spacecraft measurement since there is no STEREO A COR2 imagery. Measurement based on source candidate from GOES SUVI.
BULK measurement with no STEREO A COR2 imagery available for triangulation, assuming that the eruption is indeed on the Earth facing disk and associated with Active Region 3939 near W58.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2. This event fades after only a few frames and will therefore not be simulated.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2. This CME fades after a few frames before exiting the FOV in SOHO LASCO C2 and will therefore not be simulated.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Uncertain one-coronagraph analysis based on the above source. The width of the jet is less than 10 deg.

Measurement is based on probable source location identified from GOES SUVI imagery. The leading edge is only well-defined enough to measure for four frames in SOHO LASCO C2, so there is quite of bit of uncertainty in this measurement.
Measurement is based on probable source location identified in GOES SUVI and on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on probable source location identified in GOES SUVI and on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs.

Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not possible. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement using fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 difference imagery and source location (S17W99) based on UV imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on best fit using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery.

Measurement using source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. Some uncertainty due to extreme faintness of leading edge in STEREO A COR2 difference imagery and use of reduced-cadence frames in C3 due to comet campaign.
Measurement based on source location as well as best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit of the very fuzzy, unclear front with two coronagraphs.
One-coronagraph analysis based on the possible source location on the NW limb.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A. This CME is directed straight into the C3 pylon, which made it difficult to track the precise front beyond C2 and COR2A.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A. This CME is directed straight into the C3 pylon, which made it difficult to track the precise front beyond C2 and COR2A.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Updated measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and a few frames of STEREO A COR2 imagery starting at 2025-01-17T02:23Z, in which the CME is nearly outside the field of view.
One-coronagraph analysis with SOHO imagery only based on the location of the source eruptioin.
A very uncertain analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
One coronagraph analysis with SOHO only, based on possible source location as the AR 3964 eruption.
Measurement based on approximate source location and adjusted for best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3

These parameters are based on the best fit of the CME as seen in SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2. There is likely deflection to the north of this CME due to a large coronal hole located below the erupting active region and source location.

Parameters based on best fit of CME using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to the overlapping nature of this CME and the CME first seen in SOHO LASCO C2 at 2025-01-18T01:36Z, there is a bit of uncertainty with the parameters. The separate front of this CME is more clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery than it is in SOHO LASCO imagery, adding to the complexity of this analysis.

Single spacecraft (SOHO LASCO C2/C3) 3D measurement using the source location and faint observed deflection to the NW to get a good fit of this narrow, diffuse front which fades early in C3 in addition to traveling into the C3 pylon.

Single spacecraft (SOHO LASCO C2/C3) 3D measurement using the source location to get a fit of this wide, diffuse front, with notable difficulty due to the lack of STEREO COR2A imagery to triangulate a more accurate latitude/longitude to the slow eruption observed in SDO imagery.
One-coronagraph analysis based on the location of the source (filament eruption).
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. The source is backsided and the front of the CME is not clear/fuzzy.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Analysis based solely on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO C2/C3 and knowledge of the source location of the eruption.
Leading edge measurement, confirming far-sided analysis.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A finding a good far-sided fit.

Triangulated measurment between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, with good confidence in a matched far-sided fit around W120-W130 despite the lack of source observation in SDO AIA, GOES SUVI, or STEREO A EUVI. The brighter bulk of the front in SOHO imagery is notably dimpled inwards, but the outer edges were sharp enough to track the leading edge.
Leading edge measurement mainly relying on STEREO A but also using some late SOHO C3 frames; a poor confidence measurement of the velocity.
Measurement using late SOHO C3 frames during a SOHO C2/C3 data gap occurring during early portions of the CME.
Bulk measurement assuming modest deflection southward out of the ecliptic, though a discernible faint shock feature was associated with this event which seemed to be much wider and slower.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. This shock feature was not visible in STEREO A COR2
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This CME appears to speed up in later frames.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A.

Best fit measurement using the source location visible on the Earth-facing disk in UV imagery and using several frames of both SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Data gap in STEREO A COR2 begins at 2025-01-22T12:53Z, so only the first few frames of the leading edge are captured in COR2.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Single spacecraft measurement with SOHO LASCO C2 as it fades in the FOV, using the source location of the M2.7 from AR 13961 at S05W70 with some northward deflection.
Single spacecraft measurement with SOHO LASCO C2 as it fades in the FOV, using the source location of AR 13961 at S10W60.

Measurement based on best fit between coronagraphs and location of the candidate source with southern deflection visible in UV imagery. The leading edge of this CME appears very diffuse in STEREO A COR2 imagery and becomes too diffuse to track after only a few frames. The leading edge remains visible for longer in SOHO LASCO, about halfway through the C3 field of view.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement using source location and best fit in only SOHO LASCO C3. Not captured in STEREO A COR2 in real time; data gap in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2025-01-25T18:23Z.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO COR2A. Only uses later frames of SOHO LASCO C3 due to acceleration from earlier C3 frames and C2.

SHOCK measurement of the faint, shock-like feature extending much wider than the brighter bulk, best observed in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery. Only two usable frames of STEREO COR2A are available to triangulate this feature, and its longitude could range E40-E60 around the eruption source at E51.
BULK measurement of the brighter, inner feature of this CME. Triangulates well between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A imagery near the observed source centered around S20E51.
Analysis based on the fits with two coronagraphs and confirmed by the location of the source eruption.
One coronagraph analysis with SOHO LASCO imagery only, based on the clear location of the source eruption. There are no STEREO A coronagraph images available at this point because of the daily gap.

Measurement at W90 for the observed source in SDO AIA imagery, not a plane of sky. Triangulates between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A imagery, though notably faint and tought to discern in STEREO COR2A beacon running difference imagery.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A. Due to poor visibility of this CME in STEREO COR2A running difference imagery, and the known source at W90 in SDO imagery, this longitude could slightly range W75-W95.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. Due to the bizarre shape of this CME, especially in SOHO LASCO C2, this measurement was based on the furthest edge seen in SOHO LASCO C2 and more defined shape seen in SOHO LASCO C3
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2
Measurement using location of source observed in UV imagery and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Leading edge is very faint, especially in STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Updated measurement based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available and the measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Approximated longitude based on source location. Plausible longitude fits range from approx. W90 to W125.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2A. This event is not visible in SOHO LASCO C3 as it overlaps with CME: 2025-01-30T11:12Z.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
SHOCK measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2

Measurement based on central location of filament eruption as seen in UV imagery, and best fit of shape in SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 frames. CME not captured in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting at 2025-01-30T15:09Z.

Measurement based on location of opening field lines and brightest portion of post-eruptive arcades associated with an elongated filament eruption as seen in UV imagery, and best fit of shape in SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 frames. This CME was not captured in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting at 2025-01-30T15:09Z.

Preliminary measurement of the leading edge using only STEREO A data out to about 10 solar radii during a SOHO data outage. These parameters were later confirmed with the 2-Coronagraph measurement once SOHO became available.
Two spacecraft measurement of the leading edge; triangulation of the leading edge indicates some westward deflection occurred relative to the source region of the eruption.
Updated measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement of the bulk portion based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event has an irregularly-shaped leading edge, and the outermost feature was tracked in this measurement.
SHOCK measurement following the outermost, fainter shock feature based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location, accounting for northeast deflection, and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As this event is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Measurement based on the approximate source location, accounting for visible southern deflection, and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
One coronagraph analysis (SOHO only) in the absence of STEREO A imagery.
Measurement using best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. Agrees with source location seen in UV imagery.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. The leading edge becomes diffuse about halfway through the STEREO A COR2 field of view.
Best fit measurement using running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
One spacecraft leading edge measurement by approximating the latitude.
Best fit measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and on the location of the erupting filament.
Leading edge of the fainter shock-like portion of this CME which accelerates away from the brighter bulk using only SOHO LASCO C2.
Leading edge measurement using two spacecraft to triangulate the longitude.

SHOCK measurement using only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery, as STEREO COR2A beacon running difference imagery does not resolve this front after it resumes from a data gap at 2025-02-06T05:09Z, making triangulation within swpc_cat not possible with real-time imagery. Via visual fitting of this wide shock front, a range of E140-E155 is likely, as further longitudes in either direction make for a poor visual fit of the shock.

BULK measurement using only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery, as STEREO COR2A beacon running difference imagery does not resolve this front after it resumes from a data gap at 2025-02-06T05:09Z, making triangulation within swpc_cat not possible with real-time imagery. This measurement includes all lobes of the more dense bulk following the shock.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2. Front fades before exiting the field-of-view in SOHO LASCO C2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2

Stereoscopic best-fit measurement taken after STEREO A COR2 data gap ended. The data gap lasted from 2025-02-07T15:38Z to 2025-02-08T02:53Z. The leading edge is very faintly visible for one frame of STEREO A imagery before becoming too diffuse to discern, but fortunately, the leading edge in that one frame is clear enough to achieve a best-fit measurement between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Measurement based on potential source location. This measurement is approximate, and based on a few frames of SOHO LASCO C2.
Measurement based on source location. The CME was not measurable in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT and the potential source location as viewed from STEREO A EUV imagery.

Two-coronagraph analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs once STEREO A imagery became available. The longitude of the fit is confirmed by the location of the erupting filament (on or close to the east limb).
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. No source have been found for this event, so it is likely farsided.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measured longitude based on the approximate source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not possible. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement using SOHO LASCO C2 frames and source location. Leading edge becomes diffuse before leaving the SOHO LASCO C2 field of view.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery as well as the location of the source seen in UV imagery of the Earth-facing disk.

SHOCK measurement of a faint feature traveling ahead and slightly faster than the brighter bulk feature. Triangulated measurement with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A imagery, finding a good fit at E90, not as a plane-of-sky.

BULK measurement of the bright feature traveling slightly slower than the faint shock feature. Triangulated measurement with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A imagery, finding a good fit at E90, not as a plane-of-sky.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2

These parameters are based on the best fit of features in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is likely deflection of this CME due to the large coronal hole south of the potential source location.
Preliminary one spacecraft leading edge measurement of the CME assuming that the source on the Earth-facing disk best represents this CME.
Two spacecraft leading-edge measurement using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 to corroborate the longitude.
Two spacecraft leading edge measurement.
Single spacecraft 3D measurement using only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery due to a STEREO COR2A data gap covering this CME, using the source coordinates of N14E33 as a basis.
Single spacecraft 3D measurement using only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery due to a STEREO COR2A data gap covering this CME, using the source coordinates of S30E68 as a basis.

A very speculative measurement based on the possible but not clear source and on a very approximate fit with two coronagraph. The fit is uncertain since especially in STEREO A the front of this CME is super unclear.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs; it corresponds well to the location of the source eruption.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and corresponding well to the southern filament see in EUVI imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location. Only SOHO LASCO C2 imagery was available for a measurement, as the CME was blocked by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3.

Best fit measurement using several SOHO LASCO C3 frames and two frames of STEREO A COR2 before the leading edge leaves the COR2 field of view. This CME is partially covered by a data gap in STEREO A COR2 which lasted from 2025-02-15T16:53Z to 2025-02-16T05:23Z.
Best fit measurement using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, as well as the source location seen in UV imagery. Leading edge is too diffuse to track in SOHO LASCO C3.
Best fit measurement using an abundance of SOHO LASCO C2 frames, two frames of C3, and two frames of STEREO A COR2. The leading edge becomes diffuse after a few frames of C3 and of COR2.

Best fit measurement using candidate source location, several SOHO LASCO C2 frames, and two frames of SOHO LASCO C3. The leading edge is narrow and for most frames is completely obstructed by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3. This CME is completely covered by a data gap in STEREO A COR2 which lasted from 2025-02-16T11:38Z to 2025-02-16T15:23Z.

Measurement based on source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2. Latitude matches well, but longitude is measuring slightly east of source location; there may have been deflection due to the nearby coronal hole observed in UV imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measured longitude based on the source location. As this event is not easily observed in STEREO A COR2 imagery, a triangulated measurement was not made and the measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based off of potential source location. Parameters may be off due to limited stereoscopic viewpoints.
Measurement of shock front using only SOHO C2/C3.

Bulk measurement encompassing more of the brighter, far-southern deflected piece of the CME using SOHO C2/C3 and assuming some moderate westward deflection based on EUV signatures (i.e. about 10-15 degrees from the source).
Measurement of the leading edge using two spacecraft.
Triangulation of the leading edge using SOHO and STEREO A, which measures slightly farther east than was anticipated given the source location of the eruption.
Two spacecraft leading edge measurement using SOHO/STEREO A to triangulate the approximate longitude.
Leading edge measurement using all available imagery from two spacecraft out to approximately 18 solar radii.
Two spacecraft measurement of the leading edge using triangulation of the leading edge to determine the longitude.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 as well as general location of the complex candidate source.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 as well as location of the complex candidate source.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 as well as the location of the source filament eruption as seen in available UV imagery of the disk.
Very approximate one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO) coronagraph analysis based on the very approximate/uncertain source location (minor activity on/near the NW limb).
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs, in the absence of clear source.
One-coronagraph analysis (with SOHO LASCO), based on the location of the likely source (associated flare).
Leading edge/bulk measurement fit to the source using two spacecraft, despite STEREO A imagery being in a campaign and limiting available imagery.

Measurement of the leading edge using only SOHO C2/C3; inclusion of STEREO A COR2A data suggests the eruption originates from W55-65 but is not supported by EUV imagery. Uncertainty in longitude about 25 degrees.

One spacecraft measurement of the leading edge (SOHO), as triangulation of the leading edge with STEREO A seemed to suggest the source region was centered around W55-W65, which is not supported by EUV imagery.

Shock front measurement using only SOHO imagery, encompassing the entire width of the CME in support of solar energetic particle modeling efforts. WSA-Enlil+CONE simulations associated with this measurement are created in support of solar energetic particle modeling purposes and model outputs should not be used for CME trajectory analysis.

Bulk for trajectory analysis using only SOHO imagery focusing on mid-point of the leading edge in the northwest, the northern portion is faster than this measurement and eastern portion (portion representing the ecliptic plane) is slower than this measurement, due to the tilt of CME out of the ecliptic with respect to occulting disk.

BULK. Triangulated measurement of the brightest bulk portion of the leading edge, notably clipping some of the more faint nose off the northern portion in SOHO C3 imagery due to the uneven front. This analysis may be preliminary as further analysis continues.

Measurement based on running difference imagery from STEREO A COR2 and on-disk source location. There is significant uncertainty in this measurement due to the lack of measurable LASCO C2/C3 imagery. This CME is very difficult to distinguish from the previous CME is C2 and is obscured by the pylon in C3.

This is a SHOCK measurement. Parameters based on best fit adjusted from source location using single-spacecraft measurement method. There is no STEREO A COR2 imagery available for this event due to a data gap.

This is an updated BULK measurement with later frames. Parameters are based on the best fit of CME features based on potential source location using single-spacecraft measurement method. There is no STEREO A COR2 imagery available for this event due to a data gap.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location. CME is too faint to be reliably tracked and triangulated in STEREO A COR2.
Parameters based on best fit of brightest leading edge and bulk of CME.
Parameters based on best fit of features in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery.
Parameters are based off of the best fit of the features in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement using a very limited number of frames. Parameters based on candidate source location seen in STEREO A EUV imagery and best fit between two frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery. There is a data gap that begins in STEREO A COR2 at 2025-02-27T07:53Z and the leading edge is too diffuse to track after the gap ends at 2025-02-27T09:23Z. The leading edge becomes visible halfway through C2 field of view after the end of a data gap in SOHO LASCO C2, and once it enters the C3 field of view, it is almost entirely blocked by the pylon and cannot reliably be tracked.
Measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on approximate source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery in SWPC_CAT. STEREO A COR2 imagery was not available for this measurement.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Parameters based on the best fit of the BULK of the CME. The leading edge has a slight dimpled shape in STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Very approximate analysis of a faint wider shock mostly seen in coronagraph running difference imagery, which seems to be more farsided. This is also confirmed by the fact that the EUV wave is not seen on the Earth facing side in EUV imagery, but there are signatures of a far-sided EUV wave behind the limb, seen in AIA 171 and GOES SUVI 284 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Leading edge measurement using two spacecraft indicating strong northern deflection (out of the ecliptic). Uncertainty in latitude about +/- 10 degrees, though the longitude is well confined.
Parameters based on best fit adjusted from source location with apparent deflection to the south taken into account.

Very preliminary measurement with limited frames in SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 imagery. No STEREO A COR2 available due to data gap. The CME exhibits a sheered-type of front with a portion moving faster than other portions. These parameters are based on following the faster portion of the CME with including the slower portion within the width of the measurement. The slower portion of this CME was measuring speeds closer to 200 km/s.
Analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs. It's somewhat confirmed by the location of the flare (but there seems to be some deflection westward).
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and confirmed by the location of the likely source (C class flare).
Analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs confirmed by the location of the most likely source - the associated flare.
Analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs. Faint coronal signatures also indicate that this is a far-sided CME.
3D measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2
Parameters based on best fit of faint front in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit of the CME using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Strong deflection is likely due to nearby coronal holes.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Updated measurement with more SOHO LASCO C3 frames now available. Based on the approximate source location.

Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available and the measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
A very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. The front is very unclear/fuzzy in STEREO A COR2 and no source has been found for this event, so this analysis is very uncertain.

Measurement using the location of the source seen in UV imagery of the Earth-facing disk and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. The full leading edge and a small interior flux rope are both faintly visible in C2, but this CME is only tentatively seen as an extremely faint feature for two frames in STEREO A COR2, so there is some uncertainty with this measurement. The best fit parameters match well with the approximated source location if taking into account some southern deflection, though, which increases confidence a bit. Leading edge is too diffuse to track in SOHO LASCO C3.

Triangulated measurement using mostly SOHO LASCO C2/C3 frames, with two STEREO COR2A frames helping triangulate the longitude prior to its data gap beginning at 2025-03-05T11:53Z. This CME front diffuses out after an initial solid view in LASCO C2, it was much easier to track the leading edge in the earliest frames used for this measurement.
Analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs. There seems to be a northward and eastward deflection from the location of the source eruption.

Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2. Narrow leading edge is faint but well-defined in C2, distinguishable but not as well-defined in STEREO A COR2, and defined for only two frames in C3.

Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. Leading edge is too diffuse to reliably track in SOHO LASCO C3. This best-fit measurement corroborates the southward and westward deflection of this eruption from the source location as was suspected based on visual inspection of UV imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement tracking the northernmost feature (as seen in SOHO LASCO C2), which seems to correspond to an extremely faint loop seen in late frames of STEREO A COR2. This feature in C2 appears to be traveling slightly faster than the surrounding/overlapping material, which becomes diffuse and seems to fall apart or loose momentum before reaching the edge of the C2 field of view. The northernmost feature reaches into the SOHO LASCO C3 field of view.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement using best fit between the abundance of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 frames available. Source location with southern deflection observed in UV imagery was taken into consideration when conducting this measurement.

Best fit measurement using several frames of STEREO A COR2, four frames of SOHO LASCO C3, and two frames of SOHO LASCO C2. The leading edge is very faint in SOHO LASCO imagery; it is much more well-defined in STEREO A COR2. This CME visually overlaps with CME:2025-03-07T21:49Z in both SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2.
Leading edge of the southwest portion of the CME and not the leading edge seen more to the south in STEREO A COR2 later frames.
Leading edge measurement using only SOHO C2 as the CME is not seen well in STEREO A, possibly being edge on. This measurement assumes some modest deflection of a few degrees to the west.
Measured longitude based on the approximate source location.
Measurement based on the approximate source location, accounting for southeast deflection, and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location, accounting for southern deflection, and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement using source location seen in UV imagery and best fit between a few frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery. The leading edge is very faint in both coronagraphs and becomes too diffuse to reliably track about halfway through the SOHO LASCO C3 field of view.

Very approximate analysis as this jet was measured with the 10 deg halfwidth using the one-coronagraph method in swpc_cat, based only on SOHO imagery and on the location of the associated flare. While the jet is extremely narrow (<2 degrees half-width, lowest half-width setting in swpc_cat that we have to use is 10 degrees).
Leading edge measurement using triangulation from two spacecraft (SOHO, STEREO A) to determine a potential longitude; approximately a 20 degree uncertainty in longitude.
Leading edge measurement using only SOHO imagery and knowledge of the source eruption location to determine the longitude.
Analysis based solely on the fit with two coronagraphs.

Difficult to triangulate measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO COR2A due to the faint, wispy nature of the leading edge. Good confidence in the high southern latitude and longitude around W50-W60.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO COR2A, using only the earliest frames as the CME fades into the running difference background by 2025-03-13T06:00Z.
One spacecraft measurement (SOHO) of the leading edge assuming some modest northern deflection and fitting relative to the source observed in EUV imagery.

Measurement of wider shock front using only SOHO C2 and knowledge of the source as determined from EUV imagery. Attached WSA-Enlil+Cone simulation was executed in support of SEP models and should not be used for CME trajectory analysis. Based on simulation output, there is low confidence the CME may arrive at Earth very weakly.
Two spacecraft leading edge measurement in order to triangulate a possible longitude; uncertainty in longitude up to +/- 20 degrees.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2. This event is measuring slightly more narrow than the SWPC_CAT lower limit of 10 degrees.
Approximate analysis based solely on the fit with two coronagraphs.

Measurement made using the best fit between abundant frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery, as well as consideration of the source location seen in available STEREO A EUV, SDO AIA, and GOES SUVI imagery.

Measurement made using the best fit between abundant frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery, as well as consideration of the source location seen in available STEREO A EUV, SDO AIA, and GOES SUVI imagery.

Uncertain analysis based solely on the best possible fit with two coronagraphs. The high posts-eruptive arcades behind the west limb in STEREO A EUV imagery showing much later than the original coronal signatures of opening field lines indicate the source with the longitude >120 deg. However, no fit was found with longitudes >115 deg and the best fit was obtained with the longitude of 109 degrees. Likely deflection of about 10 to 20 degrees eastward relative to the source at W125 would be caused by a large coronal hole located to the northwest of Active Region 4012 at approximately W125.
A very uncertain analysis based on the fit of the very faint fuzzy front in both coronagraphs. No source has been found.
One-coronagraph analysis with corrected latitude of the CME (~25 degrees). Analysis is based on SOHO imagery and on the location of the likely source - faint eruption centered around (N25W10).

Measurement of the leading edge/bulk using two spacecraft out to 8 solar radii which supports westward deflection. Triangulation does not support the source location being near the original source location N35E35. Not yet seen in SOHO C3.

Very low confidence two spacecraft measurement of very faint shock front, which is hardly seen in running difference imagery. Attached WSA-ENLIL+Cone simulation was executed in support of SEP models and should not be used for CME trajectory analysis, potentially representing an overestimate of potential impacts relative to observations.

Analysis made with later SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery, leaving C2 imagery out to account for clear acceleration of the CME (from <200 km/s to >400 km/s) in later imagery. Analysis is based on the best fit in two coronagraphs, as no clear source was found on disk.  Analysis incorporates a very faint left hand side boundary of this CME as seen in later COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 and a source candidate.

Approximate one-coronagraph analysis with SOHO imagery only since the CME is not in STEREO A COR2A imagery (where it would be near center disk from the perspective of STEREO A). Analysis is based on the assumption that the source of this CME is a small filament eruption centered around N12W30 which starts around 2025-03-17T10:36Z and is best seen in SDO AIA 304.
Very approximate analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs, C2 and COR2A imagery only. The CME is too faint in C3. No clear source has been found for this CME.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. No source was found for this CME.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
Measurement based on approximate center of source dimming region and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
Measurement based on approximate center of source dimming region and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.

Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This CME appears wider in COR2A likely due to the angle of the filament that each spacecraft is viewing.
Parameters based on the best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 imagery, as well as the approximated location of the source seen in STEREO A EUV imagery. The quality and cadence of STEREO A COR2 imagery was affected during time of analysis due to a campaign scheduled to last from 2025-03-18 to 2025-03-24.

Updated measurement using the approximated source longitude of former Active Region 14018, which was estimated to be centered near west 100 degrees at the time of this event. The measured parameters may vary slightly due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement using best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 running difference and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery, as well as the approximated location of source seen in available UV imagery of the Earth-facing disk. The quality and cadence of STEREO A COR2 imagery was affected during time of analysis due to a campaign scheduled to last from 2025-03-18 to 2025-03-24.
Leading edge measurement using one spacecraft assuming the source is associated with an eruption just north of AR 4022.
StereoCAT measurement using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO COR2A due to unusable data quality of COR2A running difference imagery in the swpc_cat tool, due to an ongoing STEREO A campaign.
